title: [NO TESTS NEEDED] Change connection error to be helpful for machine users
body:
If a podman-remote connection fails, remind the user to check their linux system and podman machine vm

[NO TESTS NEEDED] 

Signed-off-by: Ashley Cui <acui@redhat.com>


Fixes: #10944

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

comments.body: Kind of silly to add two files just for a warning but it's either this with build tags or a ` if runtime.GOOS == "darwin"`

Why not just change the message to say connect to a linux system or use podman machine to create/start a Linux VM.
Podman machine works on Linux and Darwin.  Hopefully eventually on Windows.

@rhatdan does this sound better? 

cannot connect to the Podman socket, please verify the connection to the linux system, or use `podman machine` to create/start a Linux VM.
